data.title: US probes Chinese bitcoin-mining machine giant Bitmain over national security concerns: Bloomberg
data.description:
The Department of Homeland Security-led "Operation Red Sunset" examined whether Bitmain's machines could enable espionage or grid sabotage.
source: https://www.theblock.co/post/379914/us-probes-chinese-bitcoin-mining-machine-giant-bitmain-over-national-security-concerns-bloomberg?utm_source=rss&utm_medium=rss

data.title: The United States launches an investigation into Bitmain.
data.description: The United States has launched an investigation into Bitcoin mining giant Bitmain due to national security concerns. Operation Red Sunset, led by the U.S. Department of Homeland Security, is reportedly investigating whether Bitmain's mining rigs could be used for espionage or power grid sabotage in the United States. Bitmain has denied possessing any remote control capabilities, stating that the company "strictly complies with U.S. and applicable laws and regulations and has never engaged in any activities that pose a risk to U.S. national security," and calling claims that it can remotely control equipment "absolutely false." (The Block)

data.title: The Fed's leadership reshuffle drama: Trump wants Bessett, but will accept Hassett.
data.description: With Jerome Powell's four-year term ending next May—just six months away—the search for his successor as Federal Reserve chairman is in full swing, with five candidates vying for what may be the most important position in the U.S. economy. Powell has endured months of relentless attacks from Donald Trump, who has consistently demanded lower interest rates and attempted to reshape the Fed into a subservient institution. This week, Trump reiterated his desire to remove Powell, saying he "really want to fire him." The next Fed chairman may need to arm himself against Trump's scorched-earth approach—unless that person is Hassett, who has already hinted at endorsing Trump's desire for massive rate cuts. (Jinshi)

data.title: Silo Labs updates on the xUSD de-pegging incident: Approximately $2.13 million has been confirmed to have been repaid.
data.description: Silo Labs, a lending protocol project within the Sonic ecosystem, updated its xUSD de-pegging issue on the X platform. To date, confirmed repayments have reached approximately $2.13 million, including approximately $88,169 on the Sonic chain, approximately $556,669 on the Avalanche chain, and approximately $1.49 million on the Arbitrum chain. Previously, the price of Stream Finance's stablecoin xUSD deviated significantly from its target range, impacting on-chain curved markets such as Euler, Morpho, and Silo, and causing liquidity issues.

data.title: Glassnode Co-founder: The overall cycle structure remains intact; Bitcoin ETFs continue to generate net profits and their cost base remains undamaged.
data.description: Negentropic, co-founder of Glassnode, published an article on the X platform pointing out that what is happening to Bitcoin now is not a narrative shift, but a mechanism-based "buffering." Currently, ETFs are still maintaining net profits, and the cost base has not been damaged. Although some long-term holders are selling, the Solana ETF is seeing stable inflows, and altcoins are performing better than BTC and ETH. Furthermore, the overall cyclical structure remains intact, with strong demand for ETFs and spot trading, and the macroeconomy generally maintaining a neutral to bullish stance. The market has not surrendered, and the trend has not been broken. When the decline finally exhausts, the rebound may be much larger than the previous drop.

data.title: Russian Spy Ring Funded Through Crypto Laundromat, UK Police Reveal
data.description:
The UK National Crime Agency has exposed how Russian intelligence services channeled funds to a convicted espionage network through a cash-to-crypto laundering operation run by businesswoman Ekaterina Zhdanova, who attempted to bankroll former Wirecard executive Jan Marsalek’s spy ring. The revelation connects billion-dollar money laundering networks to geopolitics, organized crime, and state-sponsored activity across at least 28 British towns and cities.According to Bloomberg, nearly a year after dismantling two Russian laundromats that moved billions globally, the NCA revealed Zhdanova’s “Smart” network was used by individuals working with Russian intelligence services to fund six Bulgarian nationals now serving up to 10 years for espionage. Marsalek, who worked for Russian intelligence and disappeared during Wirecard’s 2020 collapse, spent as much as £45,000 ($58,768) on operations that included spying on journalists and politicians while plotting assassinations before the network’s dismantling
source: https://cryptonews.com/news/russian-spy-ring-funded-through-crypto-laundromat-uk-police-reveal/

data.title: Federal Reserve's Williams: Still believes there is room for rate cuts in the near term
data.description: Federal Reserve official Williams stated that he still believes there is room for near-term rate cuts; tariffs have pushed up prices, but he does not expect them to lead to sustained inflation; given the current slightly tight policy, the Fed can still cut rates in the near term; economic growth has slowed, and the labor market is gradually cooling; inflation progress has stalled, but it should be on track to reach the 2% target by 2027. (Jinshi)

data.title: A whale lost $4.66 million in principal after staking ETH for three and a half months.
data.description: According to on-chain analyst Yu Jin, a whale experienced a situation where it "earned interest but lost principal" in ETH staking. In early August, this whale withdrew 4,000 ETH from Binance and deposited it into Lido for staking. Half an hour ago, it redeemed all 4,031 ETH and transferred it to Kraken. The staking period lasted 3.5 months, earning 31 ETH (approximately $80,000) in staking rewards. However, due to the price of ETH falling from $3,905 at the time of staking to the current $2,682, the whale lost approximately $4.66 million in principal.
